# Source Card: Cytogenetic findings in Polish patients with suspected Fanconi anemia

## Summary
This peer-reviewed case-control study (100 children with clinical suspicion of Fanconi anemia [FA] plus 100 healthy controls) establishes the mitomycin C (MMC)-induced chromosomal breakage test as a first-step discriminator among FA, possible somatic mosaic FA, and non-FA marrow failure. It supplies reference magnitudes for a pediatric CDS FA-screening context: mean MMC-induced breaks/cell of 6.67 (±3.92) in FA vs 0.23 (±0.18) in non-FA, a 13% FA yield among suspected cases, and a >9x higher spontaneous-aberration rate in FA. It does not publish an explicit numeric break/cell cutoff for classification, and it explicitly requires confirmatory mutation and fibroblast testing.

- (ev_002) 13/100 (13%) FA phenotype; 27 possible mosaic FA; remainder non-FA — locator: Results/Patients — quote: "[redacted — content-rights: restricted (usage.allowed_for_public_output=false); sha256:b126e89cab19eef6b4e87efaf97fed1043f2b5514a2d2a89df437f3f64e2ace4]"
- (ev_003) MMC doses 0/50/150/300 nM, 72 h incubation — locator: Materials and methods — quote: "[redacted — content-rights: restricted (usage.allowed_for_public_output=false); sha256:cb4424ba9adb2723b925b1d0b0d59dea9a2e15ebd187a39462eb8a6a5942ac05]"
- (ev_004) Spontaneous aberrations >9x higher in FA — locator: Abstract — quote: "[redacted — content-rights: restricted (usage.allowed_for_public_output=false); sha256:0850d71476620ad4d1fdbe4ff88e6e3212e7a428b2caa0c1a88af5b92e0b898a]"
- (ev_005) Confirmatory tier required — locator: Abstract/Conclusions — quote: "[redacted — content-rights: restricted (usage.allowed_for_public_output=false); sha256:92b89fab38f4223aa3c1f5e8a07e2021b8a44282eab3111673693f408daa31f3]"

## Population / assay / lifecycle notes
- Population: children/young people with clinical suspicion of FA (hematological manifestations and/or congenital defects); age at diagnosis ranged 1 month to 32 years (median 15.36 y) — the cohort is predominantly pediatric but includes some adults, so age-band scoping matters for any CDS reuse. Both sexes; Polish national cohort.
- Assay: MMC-induced chromosomal breakage test on PHA-stimulated peripheral blood lymphocytes (RPMI 1640 + 15% FBS + gentamicin); graded MMC doses 0/50/150/300 nM for 72 h; breaks scored per cell. Values are protocol-, dose-, and scorer-dependent — not directly transferable across labs without harmonized methodology.
- Lifecycle: study published 2024-04 (Adv Clin Exp Med 2024;33(4):361-368). No guideline version; not a standard/reference-interval publication.

## Scope-exit / referral relevance (if any)
- Yes — defines a diagnostic scope boundary: a positive/ambiguous MMC test is a first-step screen only. The source explicitly states definitive differential diagnosis requires follow-up mutation testing and skin-fibroblast chromosome breakage analysis (to catch mosaicism). Any CDS rule derived from this must route "possible mosaic" and positive results to confirmatory genetic/fibroblast testing rather than terminate on the blood MMC result.

## Limitations & conflicts
- GAP: No explicit numeric breaks/cell classification cutoff (FA vs possible mosaic FA vs non-FA) is stated in the passages retrieved from this source — classification is described as pattern/interpretation-based. Threshold "breaks-per-cell cutoff for FA classification" not locatable in this source; the 6.67 vs 0.23 figures are group means, not a decision boundary. Do NOT convert the group means into an executable cutoff.
- GAP: Absolute per-group spontaneous-aberration percentages were not verbatim-quoted here; only the ">9x higher" fold-ratio (ev_004) is asserted. A secondary read (Table 1) suggested ~8.31% (FA) vs ~0.84% (non-FA) but was not captured as a verbatim passage, so those absolute values are NOT asserted.
- Cohort age span extends to 32 years (median 15.36 y); not a purely pediatric reference population — flag for age-band-sensitive reuse.
- Single national (Polish) cohort; MMC assay values are lab/protocol-dependent and not standardized reference intervals.
- No diagnosis/treatment/dosing directive is expressed or implied; this card is a PROPOSAL of source-supported facts for the CDS converter, not an executable rule.
- No direct conflicts with other cataloged sources recorded at authoring time.
